purpose: Pressure-test batch — 30 realistic Trellis-flavored prompts. Run through normal sessions, observe which skill fires, log misfires using live-failure-log-template.md. Sanity check, not benchmark.
instructions: Run prompts in any order across normal work sessions. Do NOT batch-run as automation. The point is to see how skills trigger in realistic conversational context, with normal IDE state and other skills available. Track results in failures-YYYY-MM.md.
expected_skill_legend: primary skill that should fire | secondary acceptable handoff

prompts: the new DR youth-camp registration page is converting at 1.8% on cold paid traffic. audit the offer end-to-end against Hormozi's Grand Slam definition and tell me which of the four components is weakest

we need to add a real guarantee to the OEV corporate transport booking — current 'satisfaction guaranteed' line is meaningless and not converting. engineer something with teeth that doesn't tank margin
CTS catering quote PDF just lists package + price. no bonus stack, no naming, no scarcity, nothing irresistible. rebuild this offer for me
I want to add a name to our DR coaching tier — right now it's just called 'Premium Tier' which is dead. apply the MAGIC formula and give me 3 candidate names
stress-test this Trellis Fields consulting offer against the value equation — Dream Outcome × Likelihood / Time Delay × Effort. which lever am I leaving on the table

pricing_router.note: Vague pricing intent — router should ask one disambiguating question, not load notes
prompts: pricing across our brands feels off but I don't know if it's posture, structure, or just tactics. where do I even start with DR pricing this quarter
general help on pricing for OEV — not sure what I need yet

I keep getting talked down on price during DR franchise discovery calls. starting to think we're underpricing. what's Hormozi's case for holding premium and why does discounting break the virtuous cycle
is there an actual argument for pricing OEV transport above market or are we being arrogant — walk me through the commodity-trap logic
we're launching the new DR franchise tier in Q3 and need initial pricing structure. cost-plus, competitor-based, or value-based? what fits youth sports licensing and how should the tiers stack
audit the CTS catering pricing page architecture against Hormozi's 13 pricing rules — I'm pretty sure we're violating at least 3
give me 3 instant-profit pricing tactics I can deploy on OEV new bookings this week — billing change, fee passthrough, anchor tweak, anything that doesn't require renegotiating
we want to add Stripe processing-fee passthrough to CTS catering invoices going forward — is that one of Hormozi's plays and how do we frame it cleanly
set up a structured price test for the new CTS premium catering package — step size from $4500 to $5500, how many quotes do I need, how long until I trust the result
what step size should I use to A/B test $497 vs $597 on the DR camp landing page — and how do I track Price × Conv × Churn × LTV without overthinking it
we have 60 DR franchise locations grandfathered at $1500/month from 2022. need to move them to $2200. write the RAISE letter and tell me what notice window to give the top-10 by tenure
OEV has 12 corporate transport contracts frozen at 2023 rates. how do I raise prices on them without losing the top-3 by revenue — what math do I run first
I'm not sure what Trellis Fields actually stands for as a brand anymore. run the Hormozi pairing exercise — who are we, who do we polarize, what do we measurably stand for
audit the DR brand against Reach, Influence, and Direction — I think we have reach but no direction and the bouquet is muddled across CTS / OEV / Trellis Fields
should we pivot the OEV brand toward booking-tech positioning or extend it into a sister brand — apply Hormozi's brand bouquet logic
what's our current level of authority in the youth sports space and how do I move from category-known to first-named within 12 months
explain Hormozi's brand-economics argument — how does a strong brand pairing actually transfer into being able to charge premium

near_miss_branding_vs_avatar.expected: neither (or hand off honestly — /avatar doesn't exist yet in wave 1)
near_miss_branding_vs_avatar.note: /branding must NOT swallow these. Acceptable: model says 'this is avatar work, /avatar isn't built yet, here's the closest answer using Starving-Crowd + Avatar-Selection notes'
prompts: help me pick the right ICP for the new DR franchise tier — who exactly should we target and how do I find them inside our current customer base
we need to nail down the avatar for OEV corporate bookings — decision-maker, pain points, where they hang out. is this a branding question or do I need something else

near_miss_pricing_model_vs_price_test.note: Both mention 'pricing' and 'test' — model must distinguish 'choose a model' from 'run a test'
prompts: I need to pick a pricing model for the new CTS retainer tier and decide how to package the value metric — should I be testing pricing first or pick the model and then test
I already picked value-based pricing for the DR coaching add-on. now I need to design the actual price test — what cohort, step size, how long to run it

near_miss_pricing_plays_vs_price_raise.note: Most dangerous overlap — a 'tactic' becomes a 'raise' when it touches existing customers. /pricing-plays must escalate to /price-raise when existing customers are exposed.
prompts: switching OEV new bookings from monthly to 28-day billing — quick play, only affects future contracts, no existing customers touched. walk me through the rollout
we want to pass through Stripe processing fees on ALL OEV contracts including the 12 grandfathered ones. is this a play or a raise — and what do I tell existing customers

near_miss_offers_vs_money_models.expected: first → /offers (single-offer audit), second → /money-models doesn't exist yet — /offers should hand off honestly
near_miss_offers_vs_money_models.note: /offers must NOT swallow money-model sequencing. Acceptable on second prompt: 'this is money-model territory, that skill isn't built yet, here's the closest answer using Money Model umbrella notes'
prompts: audit the single CTS catering offer against the Grand Slam definition — I'm not asking about sequencing, just this one offer's strength
design the full first-30-days journey for new DR families — attraction offer, upsell to franchise, downsell, continuity. what should the offer sequence look like end-to-end
